subject: Public Health
title: Sleeping Sickness in Colonial Africa
description: Learn Sleeping Sickness in Colonial Africa in one minute.

hook.text: Sleeping sickness killed hundreds of thousands in colonial Africa—while European powers ignored it.

buildup.text: Trypanosoma parasites, spread by tsetse flies, attack the brain and cause fatal lethargy.

discovery.text: Colonial resource extraction disrupted ecosystems, expanding tsetse fly habitats into new areas.

twist.text: Forced labor and population movements spread the disease faster than it would have moved naturally.

climax.text: Colonial medicine eventually controlled it—but only when disease threatened the economic labor supply.

punchline.text: Colonial health was about profits, not people.

quiz.question: How is sleeping sickness transmitted?
options: By tsetse flies carrying Trypanosoma parasites
